text:
Code reviews are an essential part of software development. When team members examine each other's code before it is merged, they have a much better chance of catching bugs early. Finding and fixing errors at this stage saves significant time and money compared to discovering them later in production.

Beyond finding mistakes, code reviews encourage valuable knowledge sharing. Junior developers learn from more experienced colleagues, while senior developers gain fresh perspectives. This collaboration helps spread understanding of the codebase across the entire team.

Ultimately, regular code reviews improve overall team quality. They establish shared coding standards, reduce technical debt, and build a culture of continuous improvement. A team that reviews code well together produces higher-quality software.
